question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `take(listLimit)` operator in the Flux pipeline — where `listLimit` is `Long.MAX_VALUE` when enabled — to flood the signature collection with a massive number of malicious signature files, overwhelming the `TreeMultimap` and causing memory exhaustion or processing delays that prevent legitimate signatures from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()` iterates over `sigFilesMap.keySet()` in natural order (sorted by `StreamFilename`) and that `earliestFilename` is only set once (to the first filename), to craft a scenario where a tampered file appears early in the sorted order, causing the `earliestFilename` anchor to point to the tampered file and masking the true earliest failure point in error logs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block()` call in `verifySignatures()` — which fetches the data file from cloud storage — to perform a time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) attack by replacing the data file in cloud storage between when the signature was verified and when the data file is downloaded, causing a tampered data file to be accepted under a legitimate signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Objects.requireNonNull(streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block())` call to cause a `NullPointerException` that is caught by the broad `catch (Exception e)` block, causing the verification to silently fail and fall through to the next node's signature, potentially allowing an attacker to exhaust all `CONSENSUS_REACHED` nodes and cause `verifySignatures()` to return false without any file being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `streamFile.getConsensusStart() - last.getConsensusStart()` calculation in `onVerified()` — where both values are `Long` nanosecond timestamps — to cause a long overflow or negative latency value if a tampered stream file has a `consensusStart` far in the past, corrupting the `streamCloseMetric` and potentially masking the tampered file's anomalous timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, streamFile.getConsensusEnd())` call in `onVerified()` — where `consensusEnd` is a nanosecond timestamp — to cause an `ArithmeticException` or incorrect `Instant` if a tampered stream file has an out-of-range `consensusEnd` value, causing `onVerified()` to throw an exception after `streamFileNotifier.verified()` has already been called, leaving the notifier in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate)` check in `verifySignatures()` — which disables the downloader via `downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false)` — by injecting a stream file with a timestamp far in the future, causing the downloader to be permanently disabled after processing the tampered file, halting all future Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEMO network check in `downloadNextBatch()` — where `sigFilesMap.isEmpty()` causes `downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false)` — to cause a denial-of-service on history ingestion by ensuring the signature file listing returns empty, permanently disabling the downloader on a DEMO network instance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamFileSignatureMultiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR` — which uses `Ordering.arbitrary()` (identity-based ordering) for values with the same key — to cause non-deterministic ordering of `StreamFileSignature` objects in the `TreeMultimap`, leading to unpredictable selection of which node's data file is fetched first in `verifySignatures()`, potentially favoring a tampered node's file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `verifyHashChain()` is a package-private method (not private) to directly invoke it in tests or via reflection with crafted inputs, bypassing the normal call chain and verifying that the empty hash bypass (`SHA_384.isHashEmpty`) can be triggered with a null `expectedPreviousHash`, confirming the bypass is reachable in production?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `verify()` reads `expectedPrevHash` from `lastStreamFile.get().map(StreamFile::getHash).orElse(null)` at the moment of verification — not at the moment the signature was downloaded — to cause a race condition where `lastStreamFile` is updated by a concurrent thread between signature download and data file verification, causing the hash chain check to use an incorrect expected hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `.subscribeOn(Schedulers.parallel())` in `downloadAndParseSigFiles()` to cause concurrent signature downloads from multiple nodes to race, where a slow legitimate node's signature arrives after the Flux timeout, causing it to be excluded from the consensus calculation and potentially allowing a tampered hash to reach the 1/3 threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ShutdownHelper.isStopping()` check in `verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()` — which causes an immediate return without processing remaining files — to trigger a shutdown signal at a precise moment when a tampered file's group is next in the iteration, causing the tampered file to be skipped and the hash chain to advance past it on the next startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `catch (FileOperationException | HashMismatchException | TransientProviderException e)` block in `verifySignatures()` — which logs a warning and continues to the next node — to cause all legitimate nodes to throw `HashMismatchException` (by tampering with their data files), while a malicious node's data file passes verification, causing the tampered file to be accepted as the canonical history?
